channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Chief Justice John Roberts defends judicial independence, says it is under threat in several ways
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Chief Justice John Roberts issued a defense Tuesday of judicial independence, which he said is under threat from intimidation, disinformation and the prospect of public officials defying court orders. Roberts laid out his concerns in his annual report on the federal judiciary.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: How to donate funds, blood after fatal Bourbon Street attack
item.description: As the City of New Orleans begins to recover from a fatal attack on Bourbon Street on New Year's Day, efforts are underway to help victims and their families.
item.dc:creator: Bella Dardano, Fox 5

item.title: Downtown Omaha loses power for hours after a garbage truck gets stuck in a sinkhole
item.description: OMAHA, Neb. (AP) — A large portion of downtown Omaha lost power for several hours Thursday after a garbage truck got stuck in a sinkhole where utility lines had become exposed. The truck became stuck around 8 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former top NYPD official's home searched amid allegations he demanded sex for overtime pay
item.description: Authorities searched the home of Jeffrey Maddrey, a former top NYPD official who resigned after being accused of demanding sex from a subordinate for overtime opportunities.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Firework mortars, gas cannisters stuffed inside Tesla that exploded outside Trump's Las Vegas hotel
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Firework mortars and camp fuel canisters were found stuffed into the back of the Tesla Cybertruck that exploded outside President-elect Donald Trump's Las Vegas hotel early Wednesday, killing a suspect inside the vehicle and sparking an intense investigation into possible terrorism.
item.dc:creator: TY O'NEIL and ANITA SNOW, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 1 dead, 7 injured in Tesla fire, explosion at Las Vegas Trump International Hotel in 'intentional' act
item.description: A car fire at the Trump International Hotel in Las Vegas caused smoke and loud booms, disrupting guests and nearby residents.
item.dc:creator: Linsey Lewis, NewsNation

item.title: Sugar Bowl in New Orleans postponed after deadly Bourbon Street attack
item.description: Hours following the New Year's Day tragedy on Bourbon Street, Wednesday's Allstate Sugar Bowl bout between Notre Dame and Georgia is being postponed.
item.dc:creator: Richie Mills, KRON4

item.title: Jimmy the Baptist: Carter redefined ‘evangelical,’ from campaigns to race and women’s rights
item.description: PLAINS, Ga. (AP) — Before reaching the 1978 peace deal between Egypt’s Anwar Sadat and Israel’s Menachem Begin, Jimmy Carter managed months of intense preparation, high-stakes negotiations at Camp David and a field trip to the Gettysburg battlefield to demonstrate the consequences of war.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Broccoli sold at Walmart stores in 20 states could cause sickness: FDA
item.description: A fresh vegetables and salad company has issued a precautionary advisory for some washed and ready-to-eat broccoli florets distributed at Walmart stores in 20 states, including Ohio.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Texas school district mulls building affordable housing for teachers
item.description: A Texas school district is considering partnering with a developer to build affordable housing for teachers and staff.
item.dc:creator: Sam Stark, NewsNation

item.title: Man calling on Virginia Beach to pay for damage after hitting pothole
item.description: A man is calling on the city of Virginia Beach to reimburse him for damages to his tire after he struck a pothole.
item.dc:creator: Deja Parker, NewsNation

item.title: FBI found 150 homemade bombs at Virginia home during search in December, prosecutors say
item.description: Prosecutors say federal agents found one of the largest stockpiles of homemade explosives they’ve ever seized when they arrested a Virginia man on a firearms charge last month.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Have any New Year's resolutions? Here are some tips to help make them stick
item.description: Studies have shown that up to 70% of people who make New Year’s resolutions abandon those good intentions within months. Here are some tips for how to keep those resolutions when your willpower starts to falter.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump says he'll attend Jimmy Carter's funeral
item.description: Asked by The Associated Press as he walked into a New Year's Eve party at his Mar-a-Lago club in Florida, Trump said "I'll be there.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KRON4

item.title: NYE drone show canceled on Folly Beach after drones fall from sky; at least 1 injured
item.description: FOLLY BEACH, S.C. (WCBD) - A new drone show on Folly Beach was canceled Tuesday night after the drones malfunctioned and fell from the sky. Organizers completed two drone displays before they all went down, News 2 learned.
item.dc:creator: Tim Renaud, ABC 4

item.title: Watch a replay of the 2025 Rose Parade presented by Honda
item.description: LA's Very Own KTLA 5 is proud to offer this replay of the 136th Rose Parade presented by Honda from New Year’s Day, Jan. 1, 2025. The theme of the 136th Rose Parade was “Best Day Ever!” Billie Jean King, a sports icon and equality champion, was the parade's Grand Marshal.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, ABC 4

item.title: 7 rising Democratic leaders to watch
item.description: Democrats are scrambling for a new leader to head the party and organize resistance to President-elect Trump following Vice President Harris’s loss in November's election.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, Fox 5

item.title: Alabama mother charged with the death of her 2 children, 1 year old's body still missing
item.description: An Alabama mother has been arrested and charged with the death of two children following an investigation by the 24th Circuit District Attorney's Office and the Alabama State Bureau.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Multiple people injured in balloon-drop incident at Massachusetts arcade
item.description: Cheers of excitement quickly changed to horror when the netted balloons pulled a large display down onto the crowd below, including multiple children, Storyful reports.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KRON4

item.title: Angelina Jolie, Brad Pitt reach divorce settlement after 8 years
item.description: Angelina Jolie and Brad Pitt have reached a divorce settlement, ending one of the longest and most contentious divorces in Hollywood history but not every legal issue between the two.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: FDA testing aged raw cow's milk cheese for bird flu
item.description: Raw, unpasteurized milk is a known vector of bird flu when derived from infected cow herds. It can also contain pathogens such as E. coli, salmonella, and both Streptococcus and Staphylococcus bacteria.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, Fox 5

item.title: What is 'micro-retirement?' Latest viral work trend strikes chord among burnt out workers
item.description: (KRON) -- First, there was the "great resignation," followed by "quiet quitting," and more recently, "coffee badging." But apparently, just as we prepare to ring in the new year, there's a new viral workplace trend. This time, it's called "micro-retirement.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: The year in review: Influential people who died in 2024
item.description: It was a life that took him from peanut farming to the presidency. While former President Jimmy Carter ’s time in the White House only lasted one term, the decades afterward were defined by humanitarian work that affected people all around the world.
item.dc:creator: BERNARD McGHEE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Excessive screen time leads to more anger, outbursts for preschoolers
item.description: Young children spending more than 75 minutes on a tablet were more likely to show increased outbursts of anger and frustration, a new study has found.
item.dc:creator: Kate Rix - The 74, WFLA, NBC

item.title: German chancellor takes veiled swipe at Elon Musk in New Year's address
item.description: Scholz's comments come days after the German government accused the billionaire of trying to influence its February election.
item.dc:creator: Steff Danielle Thomas, Fox 5

item.title: Former Florida deputy identified as suspect in 1979 cold case, death of his wife: sheriff
item.description: A former deputy has been identified as the suspect in a 1979 cold case that left a 25-year-old store clerk dead, the Charlotte County Sheriff's Office announced Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, KRON4

item.title: New California law takes aim at homework burden on students
item.description: You may remember spending hours after school doing homework. A lawmaker in California is hope to change that for the next generation of students in the state.
item.dc:creator: Eytan Wallace, KRON4

item.title: Trader Joe's is opening a dozen new locations in 2025: Here's where
item.description: The company’s website currently lists 12 planned locations “opening soon” across seven states and Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: In Memoriam: Those we lost in 2024
item.description: They came from every walk of life to make ours better, richer, more rounded. Here's a look at those we lost in 2024.
item.dc:creator: Tim Steele, KRON4

item.title: How to catch the Quadrantids, the first meteor shower of 2025
item.description: When the Quadrantid meteor shower peaks on Friday, it will be the year's first chance to see fireballs in the sky.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA LARSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Angelina Jolie, Brad Pitt reach divorce settlement after 8 years
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Angelina Jolie and Brad Pitt have reached a divorce settlement, ending one of the longest and most contentious divorces in Hollywood history but not every legal issue between the two.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Cities across the globe welcome the New Year in celebrations tinged with local traditions
item.description: Cities around the world are readying to ring in the New Year with celebrations highlighting local cultures and traditions, after a year roiled by ongoing conflict and political instability.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: FBI issues warning after home of NBA star Luka Doncic targeted in string of burglaries
item.description: The FBI is warning sports leagues about crime organizations targeting professional athletes following a string of burglaries at the homes of prominent NFL and NBA players.
item.dc:creator: Nancy Loo, Fox 5

item.title: Judge denies sentencing delay for ex-Sen. Bob Menendez but puts his wife's trial off until February
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Former New Jersey Sen. Bob Menendez will be sentenced on corruption charges as scheduled at the end of January, and his wife’s trial will be moved from January to February, a judge said Monday. U.S. District Judge Sidney H.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: FBI issues warning after home of NBA star Luka Doncic targeted in string of burglaries
item.description: The FBI is warning sports leagues about crime organizations targeting professional athletes following a string of burglaries at the homes of prominent NFL and NBA players.
item.dc:creator: Nancy Loo, WGN 9

item.title: Move over, Gen Alpha: 2025 marks the start of the Gen Beta era
item.description: If you just found out about Generation Alpha, brace yourself for a new crop of kids set to debut on Jan. 1.
item.dc:creator: Sakshi Venkatraman, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Democrats taunt Trump with 'President Musk' moniker
item.description: Democrats are taunting President-elect Trump with claims that billionaire entrepreneur Elon Musk is calling the shots instead of the incoming president.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WGN 9
